edges.description: Glial activation and elevated inflammatory mediators amplify nociceptive signaling in the CNS, contributing to the central sensitization phenotype.
Peripheral nociceptive input from dysfunctional C- and Aδ-fibers may sustain or amplify central sensitization via wind-up and long-term potentiation-like synaptic strengthening.

nodes.description: Augmented pain processing in the central nervous system leads to amplified pain perception. Increased excitatory neurotransmitters (glutamate, substance P) and decreased inhibitory modulation.

evidence.reference_title: Elevated cerebrospinal fluid levels of substance P in patients with the fibromyalgia syndrome.
evidence.snippet: CSF SP levels were 3-fold higher in FMS patients than in normal controls (P < 0.001)

evidence.reference_title: Possible role for glutamic acid decarboxylase in fibromyalgia symptoms: a conceptual model for chronic pain.
evidence.snippet: Glutamic acid decarboxylase (GAD) is the rate-limiting enzyme in the conversion of glutamate to GABA and decreased expression or activity of this enzyme could result in an imbalance of excitatory and inhibitory neurotransmission in the ascending and descending pain pathways.

evidence.reference_title: The Search for Biomarkers in Fibromyalgia.
evidence.snippet: Fibromyalgia is the most common of the central sensitivity syndromes affecting 2-5% of the adult population in the United States. This pain amplification syndrome has enormous societal impact

nodes.description: Glial cell activation and elevated inflammatory mediators in the CNS may contribute to central sensitization and symptom persistence.

evidence.reference_title: Evidence of central inflammation in fibromyalgia-increased cerebrospinal fluid interleukin-8 levels.
evidence.snippet: We report elevated cerebrospinal fluid and serum concentrations of interleukin-8, but not interleukin-1beta, in FM patients. This profile is in accordance with FM symptoms being mediated by sympathetic activity rather than dependent on prostaglandin associated mechanisms and supports the hypothesis of glia cell activation in response to pain mechanisms.

evidence.reference_title: Evidence of central inflammation in fibromyalgia-increased cerebrospinal fluid interleukin-8 levels.
evidence.snippet: Activation of glia cells resulting in intrathecal elevation of cytokines and chemokines has been hypothesized in chronic pain syndromes such as fibromyalgia.

evidence.reference_title: Low-grade chronic inflammation mediated by mast cells in fibromyalgia: role of IL-37.
evidence.snippet: Mast cells are involved in FM by releasing proinflammatory cytokines, chemokines, chemical mediators, and PGD2. TNF is a cytokine generated by MCs and its level is higher in FM.

evidence.reference_title: Fibromyalgia and Inflammation: Unrevealing the Connection.
evidence.snippet: Several studies highlight elevated concentrations of pro-inflammatory cytokines, dysregulation of immune responses, and neuroinflammation in fibromyalgia patients.

nodes.description: Structural and functional abnormalities of thinly myelinated Aδ and unmyelinated C-fibers affecting approximately 50% of patients (FM-SFN subtype). Reduced intraepidermal nerve fiber density (IENFD) with proximal predominant pattern contributes ongoing peripheral nociceptive input.

evidence.snippet: About 50% of women with fibromyalgia syndrome have reduced skin innervation.
